changes: OpenClaw published v2026.4.15-beta.1 on 2026-04-15 19:40 UTC, showing the anchor project is already back in beta motion after the prior stable cut.
SwarmClaw released v1.5.50 on 2026-04-15 09:32 UTC, extending its rapid orchestration/control-plane cadence.
PicoClaw posted another nightly build on 2026-04-16 02:45 UTC, keeping the cheap-hardware edge lane active.
HiClaw showed fresh official repo movement on 2026-04-16 after its recent Kubernetes-native orchestration write-up, but without a new release.
Hermes Agent kept compounding momentum with a fresh push on 2026-04-16 and no new release beyond v2026.4.13.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw continuing the preview-to-release loop is a core category signal because the anchor is still setting the pace.
SwarmClaw keeps validating the control-plane layer as a real branch of the ecosystem, not just a side experiment.
PicoClaw’s nightly cadence keeps the low-cost edge story credible even without a new stable cut.
HiClaw remains relevant in the orchestration lane, but today’s movement looked incremental rather than taxonomy-changing.
Hermes is still accumulating gravity as a learning-loop benchmark, though today’s signal was momentum rather than a strategic product-shape change.
